name: Retime the PTX wait-group and CTA barrier seam on the current 128x128 anchor

summary.name: Retime the PTX wait-group and CTA barrier seam on the current 128x128 anchor
summary.hypothesis: The latest run is already sitting on the best measured current-workload PTX surface, but the active microkernel still spends 5.93% of active warp issue slots on barrier stalls while active warps remain stuck at 16.64%. The next bounded gain is to retime the `cp.async.wait_group` plus `__syncthreads()` seam inside the 128x128 PTX microkernel so the current two-stage pipeline hands off more cleanly without growing shared memory or reintroducing the higher-register control path.
summary.expected_bottleneck: synchronization_barrier_issue layered on top of an occupancy_latency_hiding_issue in the active 128x128 PTX hot-band kernel

score_breakdown.reason: Best remaining bounded exploit on the current PTX anchor after re-anchor is already complete.

ranking_notes: This is the highest-ranked open family in the current frontier.
Barrier is no longer theoretical; it regressed versus the previous run while the rest of the profile stayed mostly flat.
The change is still local to the active PTX microkernel and does not require another restore step.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if reducing barrier stall requires extra shared-memory stages, pushes registers back above the current 198/thread level, or fails to beat the 46.532095 ms current-workload anchor.

target_hotspots.explanation: The active hot-band PTX kernel is still register-limited at 198 registers per thread.
Achieved occupancy remains pinned near the theoretical floor, so barrier work must not hurt active warps.

expected_local_changes: Reschedule the `cp_async_wait_group_0()` and `__syncthreads()` placement inside the steady-state loop.
Keep the two-stage shared-memory footprint unchanged.
Preserve the current grouped-row mapping and PTX accumulator/export path while only tightening the handoff seam.

guardrail_metrics.reason: Barrier tuning is not worth taking if latency-hiding gets worse.
The local anchor already restored the lower-register surface; barrier retiming must not give that back.
A better handoff must not simply trade barrier time for worse scoreboard stalls.

notes: Node C may now implement this one candidate.
